intro:

Rubus species, commonly known as bramble or blackberry, are a genus of thorny shrubs with edible fruits. The health benefits of Rubus species include their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Rubus species include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rubus species are teas, tinctures, and jams, which are often used to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
